The **Santa Barbara Dodgers** was the main nickname of [minor league baseball](/wiki/Minor_league_baseball "Minor league baseball") teams in the Class A [California League](/wiki/California_League "California League") based in the Pacific Ocean coastal resort city of [Santa Barbara, California](/wiki/Santa_Barbara,_California "Santa Barbara, California").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Santa_Barbara_Dodgers&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Santa Barbara Dodgers were an affiliate of the [Brooklyn Dodgers](/wiki/Brooklyn_Dodgers "Brooklyn Dodgers") (in [Brooklyn, New York](/wiki/Brooklyn,_New_York "Brooklyn, New York")) of the [National League](/wiki/National_League_(baseball) "National League (baseball)") and later their successors, the [Los Angeles Dodgers](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Dodgers "Los Angeles Dodgers") (after 1955) from 1941 to 1953 and 1964 to1967. They won the league championships in 1941, 1948 and 1951. The Dodgers entered the California League in 1964 to replace the previous [Santa Barbara Rancheros](/wiki/Santa_Barbara_Rancheros "Santa Barbara Rancheros"). The Dodgers' home stadium was [Laguna Ball Park](/w/index.php?title=Laguna_Ball_Park&action=edit&redlink=1 "Laguna Ball Park (page does not exist)").[[1]](#cite_note-Crossley-1)

The Santa Barbara Dodgers were plagued by low crowd attendances, averaging only 225 spectators per game.[[2]](#cite_note-Brantingham-2) At the end of the 1967 season, the Santa Barbara Dodgers franchise was transferred east to [Bakersfield, California](/wiki/Bakersfield,_California "Bakersfield, California").[[1]](#cite_note-Crossley-1) The Laguna Park stadium was demolished in 1970, and replaced by a parking lot,[[2]](#cite_note-Brantingham-2) and no professional minor league baseball team has been based in Santa Barbara since.[[1]](#cite_note-Crossley-1)

## Notable alumni

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Santa_Barbara_Dodgers&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Notable alumni")]

### [Baseball Hall of Fame](/wiki/Baseball_Hall_of_Fame "Baseball Hall of Fame") alumni

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Santa_Barbara_Dodgers&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Baseball Hall of Fame alumni")]

* [Sparky Anderson](/wiki/Sparky_Anderson "Sparky Anderson") (1953) inducted 2000
* [Don Sutton](/wiki/Don_Sutton "Don Sutton") (1965) inducted 1998
* [Dick Williams](/wiki/Dick_Williams "Dick Williams") (1947) inducted 2008

### Notable alumni

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Santa_Barbara_Dodgers&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Notable alumni")]

* [Jack Billingham](/wiki/Jack_Billingham "Jack Billingham") (1964) MLB All-Star
* [Paul Blair](/wiki/Paul_Blair_(baseball) "Paul Blair (baseball)") (1962) 2 x MLB All-Star; 8 x Gold Glove
* [Rocky Bridges](/wiki/Rocky_Bridges "Rocky Bridges") (1947-1948) MLB All-Star
* [Willie Crawford](/wiki/Willie_Crawford "Willie Crawford") (1964)
* [Bruce Edwards](/wiki/Bruce_Edwards_(baseball) "Bruce Edwards (baseball)") (1941) 2 x MLB All-Star
* [Alan Foster](/wiki/Alan_Foster_(baseball) "Alan Foster (baseball)") (1965)
* [Jim Gentile](/wiki/Jim_Gentile "Jim Gentile") (1952) 6 x MLB All-Star
* [Hal Gregg](/wiki/Hal_Gregg "Hal Gregg") (1941) MLB All-Star
* [Von Joshua](/wiki/Von_Joshua "Von Joshua") (1967)
* [Irv Noren](/wiki/Irv_Noren "Irv Noren") (1946) MLB All-Star
* [Wes Parker](/wiki/Wes_Parker "Wes Parker") (1963)
* [Larry Sherry](/wiki/Larry_Sherry "Larry Sherry") (1953) 1959 World Series Most Valuable Player
